points.text: Neomorph Inc. closed a $100 million Series B financing round led by Deerfield Management to advance its molecular glue degrader pipeline.

Neomorph Inc. is conducting a Phase 1/2 first-in-human trial of NEO-811 for locally advanced or metastatic non-resectable clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) as part of its molecular glue degrader program.

Neomorph Inc. will present four sessions at the 2026 AACR Annual Meeting detailing the mechanism of action and preclinical characterization of NEO-811, a first-in-class molecular glue degrader of ARNT.
Neomorph Inc. partners include Novo Nordisk, Biogen, and AbbVie across cardiometabolic, rare disease, neurology, oncology, and immunology indications to validate its molecular glue degrader platform.

Kymera Therapeutics — KT-200 — CDK2 Molecular Glue Degrader — Gilead Sciences exercised its option to license the program, advancing the first-in-class molecular glue degrader targeting CDK2 for oncology indications.

Neomorph Inc. — NEO-811 — Locally advanced or metastatic non-resectable clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) — Phase 1/2 first-in-human open-label monotherapy trial ongoing; $100M Series B financing secured to support clinical advancement.

Neomorph — NEO-811 — Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma — Four presentations scheduled at AACR 2026 (April 17-22) covering ARNT (HIF-1β) degradation mechanism, structure-based drug design, and preclinical characterization.
Kymera Therapeutics — KT-200 — CDK2 Molecular Glue Degrader — Gilead Sciences exercised option to license; program represents a first-in-class molecular glue degrader targeting CDK2 for oncology.

Neomorph Inc. — Molecular glue degrader platform development — NEO-811 and broader pipeline — $100M Series B financing closed; partnerships with Novo Nordisk, Biogen, and AbbVie established across oncology, cardiometabolic, and neurology indications.

Kymera Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader platform commercialization — KT-200 (CDK2 degrader) — Gilead Sciences exercised license option; program advances first-in-class molecular glue technology in oncology.

NEO-811 — Mechanism: Molecular glue degrader targeting previously undruggable proteins — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Patients with locally advanced or metastatic non-resectable clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) — Comparator: None (open-label monotherapy) — Endpoints: Safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and preliminary anti-tumor activity.

NEO-811 — Mechanism: Molecular glue degrader inducing targeted degradation of ARNT (HIF-1β) — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) — Endpoints: Potent single-agent activity demonstrated in preclinical models; structure-based drug design utilized for optimization.

KT-200 — Mechanism: Molecular glue-mediated CDK2 degradation — Phase: Preclinical/Early Clinical — Population: Oncology (CDK2-dependent tumors) — Status: Licensed to Gilead Sciences following option exercise.

Neomorph Inc. — Secured $100 million Series B financing led by Deerfield Management with participation from Regeneron Ventures, Longwood Fund, Alexandria Venture Investments, and Binney Street Capital to fund molecular glue degrader pipeline.
Kymera Therapeutics / Gilead Sciences — License agreement — KT-200 (CDK2 molecular glue degrader) — Gilead exercised option to license program; deal terms undisclosed in source.
